metadata.description: AVL 树: Strict Balance: The height difference between the left and right subtrees is ≤ 1. Four rotations (LL, RR, LR, RL) are used to maintain balance. Python code example for rotation in a binary search tree (BST): ```python class TreeNode: def __init__(self, key): self.left = None self.right = None self.key = key def rotate_left(node): """Performs a left rotation on the given node.""" new_root = node.right node.right = new_root.left new_root.left = node return new_root def rotate_right(node): """Performs a right rotation on the given node.""" new_root = node.left node.left = new_root.right new_root.right = node return new_root ``` This example includes two basic rotation functions, `rotate_left` and `rotate_right`, which are fundamental in maintaining balance in self-balancing binary search trees like AVL trees or Red-Black trees.

data_pointers.title: AVL 树
